name: Vibrio cholerae
priority_rationale: Epidemic-prone bacterial disease with an established vaccination and stockpile pathway.
target_scope: Human vaccines intended to prevent cholera caused by toxigenic V. cholerae, principally O1 and O139.

name: Yersinia pestis
priority_rationale: High-consequence zoonotic pathogen and medical-countermeasure priority.
target_scope: Human vaccines intended to prevent bubonic or pneumonic plague; veterinary-only records are excluded.

name: Shigella spp.
priority_rationale: Major cause of diarrhoeal disease with rising antimicrobial resistance and no routine human vaccine programme represented in this dataset.
target_scope: Human vaccines targeting one or more Shigella species or serotypes.

name: Klebsiella pneumoniae
priority_rationale: Critical antimicrobial-resistance threat and leading cause of healthcare-associated infection.
target_scope: Human vaccines intended to prevent K. pneumoniae infection; pneumococcal vaccines are explicitly excluded.

name: Invasive non-typhoidal Salmonella enterica
priority_rationale: Severe invasive disease burden, especially in African children, with S. Enteritidis and S. Typhimurium as principal vaccine targets.
target_scope: Human vaccines targeting invasive non-typhoidal S. enterica, particularly serovars Enteritidis and Typhimurium; typhoid-only records are excluded.
